title: Identity Systems

content.statement: You act like the person you believe you are.
content.question: Whose identity are you currently acting out, and is it the one you want?
content.life_lens: Wealthy, healthy, creative woman, identity drives all.
sop: Write one sentence defining the identity you want to operate from (e.g. 'I'm the type who ships daily').
Rate your Identity clarity 1-10 and Belief strength 1-10 in that identity (see calculation, page 4)
List 3 recent actions that contradicted that identity.
Pick one contradiction to eliminate this week by acting as the identity instead.
Repeat the aligned action daily until it stops requiring conscious effort.
questions: Whose identity are you currently acting out, and is it the one you want?
What would the version of you who already succeeded do differently today than you're about to do?
Where does your daily behavior contradict the identity you'd claim if someone asked you who you are?
content.equation_example: Rate Identity clarity 1-10 and Belief strength 1-10. Identity 8 times Belief 7 equals 56 out of 100. Higher means your actions will reliably match the identity you claim. Lower means a gap between who you say you are and what you actually believe, so your actions default somewhere else.
content.closing_statement: Identity isn't declared once, it's proven by repetition.

caption:
Principle Nº 136: Identity Systems

You act like the person you believe you are.

Ask yourself: Whose identity are you currently acting out, and is it the one you want?
